2604.04456.title: Empirical Characterization of Rationale Stability Under Controlled Perturbations for Explainable Pattern Recognition
2604.04456.paper_summary: Reliable pattern recognition systems should exhibit consistent behavior across similar inputs, and their explanations should remain stable. However, most Explainable AI evaluations remain instance centric and do not explicitly quantify whether attribution patterns are consistent across samples that share the same class or represent small variations of the same input. In this work, we propose a novel metric aimed at assessing the consistency of model explanations, ensuring that models consistently reflect the intended objectives and consistency under label-preserving perturbations. We implement this metric using a pre-trained BERT model on the SST-2 sentiment analysis dataset, with additional robustness tests on RoBERTa, DistilBERT, and IMDB, applying SHAP to compute feature importance for various test samples. The proposed metric quantifies the cosine similarity of SHAP values for inputs with the same label, aiming to detect inconsistent behaviors, such as biased reliance on certain features or failure to maintain consistent reasoning for similar predictions. Through a series of experiments, we evaluate the ability of this metric to identify misaligned predictions and inconsistencies in model explanations. These experiments are compared against standard fidelity metrics to assess whether the new metric can effectively identify when a model's behavior deviates from its intended objectives. The proposed framework provides a deeper understanding of model behavior by enabling more robust verification of rationale stability, which is critical for building trustworthy AI systems. By quantifying whether models rely on consistent attribution patterns for similar inputs, the proposed approach supports more robust evaluation of model behavior in practical pattern recognition pipelines. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/anmspro/ESS-XAI-Stability.
2604.04456.paper_summary_zh: 可靠的模式識別系統應該在類似輸入之間展現一致的行為，並且其解釋應保持穩定。<lb/>然而，大多數可解釋的人工智慧評估仍然以實例為中心，並未明確量化歸因模式在共享相同類別或代表相同輸入的小變化的樣本之間是否一致。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們提出了一種新穎的指標，旨在評估模型解釋的一致性，確保模型能夠一致地反映預期目標，並在保持標籤的擾動下保持一致性。<lb/>我們使用預訓練的BERT模型在SST-2情感分析數據集上實現了這一指標，並在RoBERTa、DistilBERT和IMDB上進行了額外的穩健性測試，應用SHAP計算各種測試樣本的特徵重要性。<lb/>所提出的指標量化了具有相同標籤的輸入的SHAP值的餘弦相似度，旨在檢測不一致的行為，例如對某些特徵的偏見依賴或未能為類似預測保持一致的推理。<lb/>通過一系列實驗，我們評估了這一指標識別不對齊預測和模型解釋不一致性的能力。<lb/>這些實驗與標準的忠實度指標進行比較，以評估新的指標是否能有效識別模型行為何時偏離其預期目標。<lb/>所提出的框架通過使得對理由穩定性的更穩健驗證成為可能，提供了對模型行為的更深入理解，這對於構建可信的人工智慧系統至關重要。<lb/>通過量化模型是否依賴於類似輸入的一致歸因模式，所提出的方法支持在實際模式識別管道中對模型行為進行更穩健的評估。<lb/>我們的代碼可在 https://github.com/anmspro/ESS-XAI-Stability 上公開獲得。
2604.04456.author: Abu Noman Md Sakib et.al.
2604.04456.authors: Abu Noman Md Sakib, Zhensen Wang, Merjulah Roby, Zijie Zhang
